The Senate Intelligence Committee report on Russian interference in the United States presidential election, officially titled Report of the Select Committee on Intelligence United States Senate on Russian Active Measures Campaigns and Interference in the 2016 U.S. Election, is the official report in five volumes documenting the findings and conclusions of the United States Senate Intelligence Committee concerning the Russian attack efforts against election infrastructure, Russia's use of social media to affect the election, the U.S. government's response to Russian activities, review of the Intelligence Community Assessment, and counterintelligence threats and vulnerabilities. The redacted report is 1,313 pages long. It is divided into five volumes.  The first volume of the report was released on July 25, 2019, and the fifth and last volume was released to the public on August 18, 2020.[1][2] The Senate Intelligence Committee's investigation extended more than three years, includes interviews of more than 200 witnesses, and reviews more than one million documents.[2] Marco Rubio, acting committee head,[a] said that "no probe into this matter has been more exhaustive.”[4] On the stature of the report, the Senate Intelligence Committee said the report is "the most comprehensive description to date of Russia's activities and the threat they posed".[5]  The Republican-led Senate Intelligence Committee submitted the first part of its five-volume report in July 2019 in which it concluded that the January 2017 Intelligence Community assessment alleging Russian interference was "coherent and well-constructed". The first volume also concluded that the assessment was "proper", learning from analysts that there was "no politically motivated pressure to reach specific conclusions". The final and fifth volume, which was the result of three years of investigations, was released on August 18, 2020,[6] ending one of the United States "highest-profile congressional inquiries."[7][8] The Committee report found that the Russian government had engaged in an "extensive campaign" to sabotage the election in favor of Donald Trump, which included assistance from some of Trump's own advisers.[7]  L

"These remain real concerns, but the past few weeks have revealed an overlooked threat that similarly speaks to a loss of control. While Canadians have been worried about others controlling our infrastructure or using our data, we have lost sight of the risks of Canada being locked out of the most capable artificial-intelligence models, with consequences that could leave the country in the second tier of AI.  Last month, a U.S. export-control directive forced Anthropic, the company behind the popular Claude AI service, to suspend access to its two most advanced models for every customer worldwide, Mythos and Fable. After two weeks of negotiations, the U.S. government lifted the controls last week, restoring the flagship Fable model globally, while the most powerful version remains limited to government-approved organizations. Meanwhile, OpenAI’s newest model is being rolled out, with the U.S. government approving access one customer at a time."

🇫🇷 France's Atout France Allegedly Compromised via Critical IDOR Vulnerability  A threat actor claims to have exploited a critical Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability affecting Atout France, the French tourism development agency, resulting in the alleged compromise of more than 400,000 user accounts.  According to the forum post, the attacker claims the exposed data includes:  * Profile IDs, Drupal user IDs, and member numbers. * Names, email addresses, phone numbers, and organizational details. * SIRET business identifiers and user roles. * Login information and profile metadata. * Billing and delivery addresses, including postal codes, cities, and countries. * The actor also claims several administrative accounts were modified following the intrusion.  Analyst Note: These claims have not been independently verified. If authentic, the incident would highlight the impact of IDOR vulnerabilities, which can allow unauthorized access to sensitive records through insufficient access control. Organizations should promptly investigate the claims, validate administrative account integrity, and review authorization controls across affected applications.  #DDW #DarkWeb #Intelligence #France

Grozev Reports Release in UK of Two "Spies" Who Tracked Him and Dobrokhotov  After serving the mandatory half of their prison sentences, two individuals convicted in a Russian espionage case have been released in the United Kingdom. This was reported by investigative journalist Christo Grozev, whom a court previously identified as one of the group's targets alongside Roman Dobrokhotov, the editor-in-chief of The Insider.  According to Grozev, neither he nor Dobrokhotov was notified of the convicts' release.  In May 2025, a London court sentenced six Bulgarian nationals to prison terms ranging from five to ten years. The investigation established that the ring operated across several European countries, tracking Grozev and Dobrokhotov and discussing plans for their kidnapping or assassination.  The prosecution asserted that the group's activities were coordinated by Orlin Roussev, who was linked to former Wirecard top executive Jan Marsalek, currently believed to be in Russia. The court also concluded that the defendants' actions posed a threat to press freedom.  Christo Grozev is a Bulgarian investigative journalist and the former executive director of Bellingcat. He is well known for his investigations into the poisoning of Alexei Navalny, the Sergei Skripal case, and the activities of Russian intelligence services. In recent years, Grozev has repeatedly stated that he considers himself a primary target for Russian intelligence and "Putin's enemy No. 1." In 2025, a documentary about him titled "Antidote" was released.   Photo: SOTA

🚨 Rusya bağlantılı olduğu değerlendirilen APT-C-20 (Fancy Bear), yeni saldırısında shellcode'u normal görünen PNG dosyalarının içine gizleyerek bellekte çalışan (fileless) bir C# arka kapısı yüklüyor. Saldırıda COM hijacking, steganografi ve bulut tabanlı C2 iletişimi birlikte kullanılıyor.  Peki bu saldırı nasıl yapılıyor?  Saldırganlar, makrolu bir Word belgesi aracılığıyla sisteme bir DLL ve normal görünen bir PNG dosyası bırakıyor. DLL, PNG'nin içine gizlenmiş shellcode'u bellekte çalıştırarak dosyasız (fileless) C# arka kapısını yüklüyor. Böylece zararlı yazılım diske kaydedilmeden çalıştırılıyor ve tespit edilmesi zorlaşıyor.

sample_texts: July 8, 1942. Erwin Rommel is closer to winning the entire war in North Africa than he will ever be
North West is a middle schooler and I think all of this is really sad.

sample_tweets.text: July 8, 1942. Erwin Rommel is closer to winning the entire war in North Africa than he will ever be again, and he cannot move an inch.  Think about how he got here. He had just smashed the British at Gazala, taken the fortress of Tobruk in a single day, and chased the beaten Eighth Army hundreds of miles back across the desert into Egypt. In Cairo the British were burning secret papers in the streets. The Royal Navy was pulling out of Alexandria. People started calling it the Flap. It really looked like Egypt, the Suez Canal, and the oil beyond it were about to fall.  And then Rommel hit the one piece of ground where all his genius stopped working. A dusty railway halt called El Alamein, about 150 miles short of Cairo. To the north was the Mediterranean. To the south was the Qattara Depression, a vast sunken pit of soft salt and cliffs that no army could cross. Just a narrow 40 mile bottleneck in between, with nowhere to sweep around the flank the way he always did.  The man waiting for him was Claude Auchinleck, the British commander in chief, who had sacked his own field general and taken personal command of the battle when everyone else expected a collapse. All through July he counterpunched, and he did something clever. Instead of trading blows with the German armor, he went hunting the weaker Italian divisions holding the line, cracking Rommel's army where it was softest and forcing the panzers to keep rushing to plug the holes.  Neither side could land the knockout. Both armies were exhausted, out of fuel, running on fumes and stubbornness. But the line held. Rommel never got past it. No pyramids for the Desert Fox, no Suez, no oil.  This is the battle that quietly saved Egypt in the summer of 1942. And the general who actually stopped Rommel got fired six weeks later, right before Montgomery took over the same ground, fought on the same line, and walked away with all the fame. History is funny like that.

400 years of Earth's magnetic North Pole wanderings 🧭  For centuries, Earth’s magnetic north pole has been slowly drifting — but in recent decades, its movement has accelerated dramatically.  Historical records dating back to the early 1600s show the pole gradually migrating from the Canadian Arctic toward Siberia. For most of that time, it moved at a modest pace of about 10 miles (16 km) per year.  Since the mid-20th century, however, the speed has surged — now racing at nearly 35 miles (56 km) per year. In 2017, it crossed the International Date Line, and it continues its rapid journey toward Russia.  What’s driving this sudden acceleration?  Deep beneath the surface, in Earth’s outer core — an immense ocean of molten iron roughly 1,800 miles (2,900 km) below our feet — swirling currents of liquid metal generate the planet’s magnetic field through dynamo action. Changes in these flows can tug the magnetic poles in new directions.  Currently, the molten iron is churning more vigorously beneath Siberia than under Canada, pulling the magnetic north pole faster toward the east.  Unlike the fixed geographic North Pole, the magnetic pole is never truly stationary — it can shift slightly from day to day. But the long-term trajectory, reconstructed from 400 years of data including old ship logs, compass readings, volcanic rocks, and modern satellite observations, reveals a clear and accelerating trend.  Why does this matter?  Compasses point to magnetic north. Rapid shifts affect:  · Airport runway designations  ·Smartphone compass sensors  · GPS backup systems  · Navigation models used worldwide  · Even the migration patterns of animals like whales, birds, and sea turtles that rely on Earth’s magnetic field for orientation  As the pole races onward, global navigation databases must be frequently updated to keep pace with its unpredictable sprint.

sample_tweets.text: Tesla: "At Gigafactory Texas, we are planning to implement a waste heat recovery system in our data center. This system captures waste heat and repurposes it to provide process heating water to our vehicle coatings and paint shops, reducing the need for water from our chiller plants.  We have also made significant improvements to the energy efficiency of our chillers. During colder and drier months, we reduce the condenser water temperature, helping us save energy while maintaining optimal cooling performance.  We can balance the short-term environmental impacts of our data centers with the long-term benefits they provide by supporting our products' avoided emissions and getting us closer to a sustainable and abundant future."

I’m not saying pregnancy discrimination lawsuits are never winnable. I’m saying they are often hard to win in U.S. court because the worker usually has to prove pregnancy or protected leave caused the adverse action. Federal FMLA rules even allow layoffs during leave if the employer can prove the person would have been laid off anyway. That is different from Europe-style protections. And the data backs this up: federal employment discrimination plaintiffs historically win far less often than other civil plaintiffs, and pregnancy accommodation plaintiffs historically lost more than two-thirds of post-Young v. UPS cases.

The EEOC data shows many discrimination claims do not end favorably for workers  In FY2025, the EEOC processed 88,201 new discrimination charges and resolved 90,743 charges. Only 17.5% of resolved charges had “merit factor resolutions,” meaning outcomes favorable to the charging party.

Jadi kalo liat data dari OJK emang mengkhawatirkan, bersanding lurus dengan kekhawatiran "bankir" yang seharusnya lebih "terasa" disitu ada macet kredit.   ​Jadi secara total, kredit macet (NPL) bank itu ada di 2,17% an. Tapi kalau dibongkar, segmen UMKM itu naik tinggi banget ke 4,36% & multifinance (leasing) naik ke 2,87%.  ​Kok bisa?  Jawabannya karena ​efek "Bantalan" Hilang, jadi waktu Covid kemarin, ada relaksasi (kebijakan kredit rada dilonggarkan atau gampangnya saat itu, catat kredit telat sebagai 'lancar'). Begitu kebijakan ini selesai, borok aslinya langsung kelihatan.  ​Fenomena "Makan Tabungan", pasti tau kan berita belakangan, banyak PHK & harga barang pokok naik, nah ini buat kelas menengah kehabisan tabungan. Akhirnya, cicilan terpaksa dikorbankan atau dibiarin mandek.   ​Dampaknya? Bank jd super pelit kasih pinjaman baru (kredit UMKM cuma tumbuh 0,6%!). Kondisi riil di akar rumput lagi megap-megap, makanya bankir panik karena ini bukan siklus bisnis biasa. Ini efek domino dari kebijakan sebelumnya.   Maaf kalo salah, sedikit share pengetahuan aja.

SUPER TYPHOON INDAY UPDATE (JULY 8, 11 P.M.)  Bahagyang humina ang Super Typhoon #IndayPH habang kumikilos patungong kanluran, hilagang-kanluran sa Philippine Sea, batay sa 11 p.m. update ng DOST-PAGASA ngayong Miyerkules, July 8.  Huling namataan ang sento ng Super Typhoon sa layong 1,035 km silangan ng Northern Luzon. Taglay nito ang lakas ng hangin na 185 km/h, at bugsong aabot sa 230 km/h.  Nakataas ang Tropical Cyclone Wind Signal No. 1 sa sumusunod na lugar:  Luzon • Batanes • Cagayan including Babuyan Islands • Isabela • Northeastern portion of Quirino (Maddela) • Apayao • Northern portion of Abra (Tineg, Lagayan, Danglas, San Juan, Lagangilang, Licuan-Baay, Malibcong, Lacub, Daguioman, Dolores, La Paz) • Kalinga • Eastern portion of Mountain Province (Natonin, Paracelis) • Eastern portion of Ifugao (Aguinaldo, Alfonso Lista) • Ilocos Norte • Northern portion of Aurora (Dilasag, Casiguran) • Northern and central portions of Catanduanes (Pandan, Caramoran, Bagamanoc, Panganiban, Viga, Gigmoto, Baras, San Miguel)  Batay sa forecast, lalabas ng Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR) ang bagyo sa Sabado, July 11. Magla-landfall it sa eastern coast ng mainland China sa Linggo, July 12.  📸: DOST-PAGASA

WEATHER UPDATE: SUPER TYPHOON 'INDAY' NASA EXTREME NORTHERN LUZON SA HULYO 10 - PAGASA  Iniulat ng Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) na inaasahang hihina ang bugso at lakas ng hangin ng super typhoon "Inday" bago lumabas ng Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR) sa Sabado Hulyo 11.  Ayon sa inilabas na weather bulletin alas-5 ng hapon ngayong Miyerkules, Hulyo 8, kikilos ang "Inday" patungong Extreme Northern Luzon pagsapit ng Biyernes ng gabi, Hulyo 10 o Sabado ng umaga, Hulyo 11, bago tumawid sa southern islands ng Japan at hilagang bahagi ng karagatan sa Taiwan.  Sa kasalukuyan, kumikilos ang bagyo sa bahagi ng Northern Luzon kung saan nanatili pa rin nakataas ang Tropical Cyclone Wind Signal (TCWS) No. 1 sa mga sumusunod na lugar.  Ilocos Norte,  Apayao,  Northern portion ng Abra (Tineg, Lagayan, Danglas, San Juan, Lagangilang, Licuan-Baay, Malibcong, Lacub, Daguioman, Dolores, La Paz),  Kalinga,  Eastern portion ng Mountain Province (Natonin, Paracelis), the eastern portion of Ifugao (Aguinaldo, Alfonso Lista),  Batanes, Cagayan including Babuyan Islands, Isabela, the northeastern portion of Quirino (Maddela),  Northern portion ng Aurora (Dilasag, Casiguran),  Northern at Central portions ng Catanduanes (Pandan, Caramoran, Bagamanoc, Panganiban, Viga, Gigmoto, Baras, San Miguel).  #PilipinasToday #PAGASA #WeatherUpdatePH #BagyongInday #IndayPH

Bahagyang lumakas si Super Typhoon #IndayPH habang kumikilos sa direksyong West Northwestward sa bahagi ng Philippine Sea, ayon sa 11:00 a.m. update ng #PAGASA ngayong araw, Hulyo 8.   Huling namataan ang sentro ng bagyo sa layong 1,270 km silangan ng Northern Luzon taglay ang lakas ng hangin malapit sa gitna na 195 km/h at pagbugsong aabot sa 240 km/h.   Kasalukuyang nakataas ang Wind Signal No. 1 sa Batanes; Cagayan, kabilang ang Babuyan Islands; Apayao; Kalinga; Isabela; hilagang bahagi ng Aurora; hilaga at gitnang bahagi ng Catanduanes; hilagang-silangang bahagi ng Quirino, at ilang mga lugar sa Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Mountain Province at Abra. 🚨 11.4M Mexico City Citizen Records Allegedly Leaked on Darkweb Forum  A darkweb forum post claims the exposure of a database containing personal information for over 11 million residents of Mexico City. The listing, posted by an actor using the handle 'moonsh1n3', alleges that the dataset includes sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) such as full names, voter IDs, dates of birth, and complete residential addresses.  According to the post, the database contains 11,482,760 records in TXT format. The exposed data fields reportedly include:  *   Voter ID (Clave Elector) *   Full Name *   Date of Birth *   Gender *   Occupation *   Full Residential Address (Street, Number, Colony, Postal Code) *   INE Registration Date  The samples provided in the thread show detailed address information, including street names, internal/external numbers, and specific colonies within Mexico City. The actor claims this data covers individuals residing in the capital region.  Mexico City is one of the most populous metropolitan areas in the world, with a population exceeding 9 million in the city proper and over 22 million in the greater metropolitan area. A breach of this scale would represent a significant exposure of citizen data, potentially facilitating identity theft, phishing campaigns, and physical security risks for affected individuals. The source of the leak was not specified in the post, nor was any ransom demand or attribution to a specific threat group mentioned.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by Mexican authorities or cybersecurity firms.

🚨 Alleged Azazie Database Leak Exposes SSNs and Payment Data  A darkweb forum post claims to have obtained a database from the US-based fashion retailer Azazie, allegedly containing sensitive customer information including Social Security Numbers (SSNs) and credit card details. The listing, posted under the title "azazie.com Database USA," asserts that the stolen SQL file includes user purchase history, order details, and financial data.  According to the post, the exposed dataset contains the following types of information:  *   User purchase records *   Order details *   Credit card information *   Social Security Numbers (SSNs)  Azazie is a prominent online marketplace for custom-made dresses and formal wear, operating primarily in the United States. The company has grown significantly in recent years, serving millions of customers for weddings, proms, and other special events. A breach of this magnitude would be particularly damaging due to the inclusion of SSNs, which are high-value targets for identity theft.  The actor behind the leak provided a download link via a file-hosting service and listed a Telegram handle for further contact. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by Azazie or third-party security researchers.

⚠️ Threat Actor Claims 430M Records Leaked from Argentine Credit Bureau InfoExperto  A darkweb forum post alleges the exposure of over 430 million records from InfoExperto, a major Argentine credit bureau and data broker. The actor claims to have dumped partial source code, VPN keys, SSH root keys, and production environment configurations containing hardcoded credentials from the company's servers.  According to the listing, the breach includes sensitive government-issued data and financial records, including:  *   **Payroll/Employment Records:** Over 100 million links between employees, employers, and salaries (AFIP Form 931) through April 2026. *   **National Identity Registry:** 65 million records from the AFIP padron, including full names, DNI, DOB, addresses, and status. *   **Telecom Data:** Approximately 176 million cell phone records across Personal/Telecom, Claro, and Movistar, linking names, DNIs, and addresses to phone lines. *   **State-Declared Contacts:** 86 million phone records and 30 million email records sourced from ANSES, IFE, and other government programs. *   **Financial Data:** 245,000 Banco Columbia credit cardholders with full 16-digit PANs, plus 4,675 platform-stored cards with CVVs and expiry dates in plaintext. *   **Biometric Data:** 48,000 KYC identity verifications with face match scores and raw ID card chip data.  The post further claims the leak includes AFIP digital certificates that could allow impersonation of the company to query national identity registries directly. It also lists internal scrapers used to pull live data from entities such as AFIP, ANSES, BCRA, and Movistar.  InfoExperto is a significant player in Argentina’s financial infrastructure, providing credit scoring and identity verification services to banks, fintechs, and law firms. The alleged exposure of plaintext CVVs and biometric data poses severe risks for identity theft and financial fraud if the data is authentic.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by InfoExperto or security researchers.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of 100M Bumble User Records on Darkweb  A post on an underground forum alleges the availability of a database containing over 100 million unique records from Bumble, a major dating application. The actor claims to be offering the dataset, which reportedly includes detailed personal information and behavioral data of users.  According to the listing, the exposed data fields include:  *   User identifiers (user_id, profile_id) *   Contact information (phone numbers, email addresses) *   Personal details (first name, date of birth, gender, sexual orientation) *   Location data (city, region, country) *   Profile attributes (bio, interests, education level, job title) *   Behavioral metrics (swipe counts, match counts, conversation counts) *   Account status and premium tier information  Bumble is a significant player in the online dating sector, with tens of millions of monthly active users globally. The exposure of such a large volume of sensitive personal data, including phone numbers and precise location history, could facilitate targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and harassment if the data is authentic.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by security researchers or confirmed by Bumble.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 430M Record Breach of Argentine Credit Bureau InfoExperto  A darkweb forum post reveals that a threat actor has allegedly dumped over 430 million records from InfoExperto, a major Argentine credit bureau and data broker. The actor claims to have exfiltrated not only the core database but also sensitive server artifacts, including partial source code, SSH root keys, VPN credentials, and production environment configurations containing hardcoded secrets.  According to the listing on the underground forum, the breach includes high-value government-linked datasets and financial records. The alleged data dump contains:  *   **Payroll and Employment Records:** Over 100 million links between employees, employers, and salaries (AFIP Form 931) through April 2026. *   **National Identity Registry:** 65 million records from the AFIP padron, including full names, DNI numbers, dates of birth, addresses, and economic activity status. *   **Telecommunications Data:** Approximately 176 million cell phone records from providers including Personal/Telecom, Claro, and Movistar, linking phone numbers to names, DNIs, addresses, and plan details. *   **State-Declared Contact Info:** 86 million phone records and 30 million email records sourced from government programs such as ANSES, IFE, Alimentar, and ENACOM, updated through June 2026. *   **Financial Data:** 245,000 Banco Columbia credit cardholders with full 16-digit Primary Account Numbers (PANs), plus 4,675 platform-stored cards with CVVs and expiry dates in plaintext. *   **Biometric Data:** 48,000 KYC identity verification sessions with face match scores and raw ID card chip data. *   **Infrastructure Keys:** AFIP digital certificates that allegedly allow impersonation of the company to query the national identity registry directly.  InfoExperto is a significant player in Argentina's financial infrastructure, providing credit scoring and risk management services to banks, fintechs, and other enterprises. The exposure of such granular personal and financial data poses severe risks of identity theft, fraud, and targeted social engineering attacks against millions of Argentine citizens. The post also lists 18 million ART (worker compensation) records and billing history for 62,000 corporate clients.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by InfoExperto or Argentine authorities.

🚨 BONK DAO Loses Approximately $20 Million in Treasury Funds Due to Malicious Vote  A malicious DAO governance proposal targeted BonkDAO, which is part of the BONK community in the Solana ecosystem. by a malicious DAO governance proposal. BONK’s official X account announced that, as a result of the incident, approximately $20 million worth of BONK tokens were drained from the BonkDAO treasury, and the exchange wallets used to purchase BONK prior to the attack have been identified. It was noted that BonkDAO is working with exchanges, bridge services, and the Solana Foundation and that law enforcement has been notified.  A DAO is a governance model in which a project’s decisions A DAO operates as a governance model where token holders vote on project decisions instead of a central team, through token holders’ votes rather than a central team's. Realms is one of the platforms within the Solana ecosystem where such DAO votes take place. At the center of the incident was a proposal titled “BIP #76 - Sowellian BonkDAO” created on Realms. The proposal included statements such as “implementation of Sowellian governance, the establishment of new members and a council, rebuilding the community, evaluation of assets, and that those who vote ‘YES’ will be eligible to receive tokens.”  However, a critical part of the proposal instructed that 4,426,104,450,305 BONK from the BonkDAO treasury be sent to the address 9bxWkNf3BtJ6iehq9KbX9uCWMjem4TFiPZ19T2sYJHvQ. While the vote required a 1% quorum, the 882.38B BONK “Yes” votes narrowly surpassed the 879.95B BONK quorum threshold. As a result, the proposal was approved with a 99.9% “Yes” vote. This incident is not considered a classic smart contract hack, but rather a governance attack. A governance attack occurs when an attacker gains voting power and manipulates the DAO’s decision-making mechanism to their own advantage instead of technically breaching the system. In this case, it appears that the attacker acquired enough voting power to influence the vote by purchasing BONK prior to the proposal and then passed the proposal containing the malicious transfer instruction.  For this reason, DAO users must carefully review not only the proposal title or description but also the instruction details, the amount of tokens to be sent, the destination address, and any interactions with the treasury. On the DAO side, additional security measures such as high quorum requirements, timelocks, multisig approval, and emergency veto mechanisms are critical for large treasury transfers.  #GovernanceAttack #Web3Security #ThreatMon #TokenGovernance #DigitalAssetSecurity

🚨Alleged US Personal Data Leak Exposes SSNs and Bank Details  A darkweb forum post claims that 153,210 records of US personal data have been compromised, exposing highly sensitive financial and identity information. The listing, dated July 7, 2026, alleges the breach occurred on May 7, 2026, and offers the dataset for sale at a price of $150.  According to the post, the leaked database contains a wide range of personally identifiable information (PII) and financial data. The actor lists the following fields as exposed:  *   First Name *   Last Name *   Email Address *   Physical Address (Street, City, State, Zip) *   Phone Numbers *   Bank Account Numbers *   Routing Numbers *   Social Security Numbers (SSN) *   Date of Birth *   Driver's License Numbers  The sample data provided in the thread includes entries from various US states, including Illinois, Indiana, Arizona, Tennessee, Arkansas, Michigan, Kentucky, New York, Alabama, California, Colorado, Massachusetts, South Carolina, and Texas. The inclusion of Social Security numbers, bank account details, and driver's license numbers suggests a high risk of identity theft and financial fraud if the data is authentic.  The post does not identify the source of the breach or the specific organization targeted. It only provides a Telegram handle for contact, which has been omitted for safety. The combination of SSNs and banking credentials makes this type of data particularly valuable on the black market for synthetic identity fraud and direct account takeover attacks.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, nor has any specific company claimed responsibility or confirmed the incident.

sample_tweets.text: In regards to these files inside of "child porn folder", the files present inside all end in *.lnk (A Windows shortcut). This is a very common file masquerading technique to give the facade of a legitimate file, but it's actually malware (shortcut pulls a file that it detonates, or points to an actual malware payload).  However, there is a small chance this is not file masquerading and a legitimate child predator accidentally uploaded files which are flagged as malware due to them linking to an external domain... or something else.  Basically, there are a few things this could be  1. Legitimate CSAM (Child sexual abuse material) 2. Malware payload disguised as CSAM to target pedophiles 3. Malware naming itself something CSAM related to avoid analysis  Regardless, for legal reasons (and emotional), I won't even try to figure out.

Yesterday two different people contacted me about two different projects on GitHub.  "Tito" DMd me about someone making a comment on GitHub claiming to have a patch for "synara" (Image 1)  "Robert Z" emailed about something similar on a different GitHub project (image 2).  Interesting.  Upon further investigation it turns out this exact file has been discovered online named: - hb_patch_v1112 - registry_patch_v0.1.7 - rep_fix_v1.zip  - sodium_fix_v1 - log_fix_patch blah blah blah (image 3)  Basically, someone is creating accounts on GitHub, going to open issues, and leaving a comment saying they've found a patch (it's likely automated, but whatever). The patch is malware.  It appears this campaign began around July 7th (yesterday) and is making its rounds pretty quickly.  When you look inside (hehe silly reference) it's a program written in Go. When the binary detonates it queries a remote host which resolves to ... Telegram. The Telegram channel description specifies a website. The website in the Telegram description is where the payload exfiltrates code too.  They're doing it this way because they can quickly change the Telegram channel description if the website they specify is taken down. It's basically a bootleg DNS resolver (image 4).  Anyway, this is StealC (based on YARA identifiers). This is (probably) a new malware campaign by someone using StealC. I like it. Very cool usage of GitHub spam combined with a bootleg ass DNS resolver on Telegram. It probably helps because I doubt Telegram is quick to take action with takedown requests.

> be me > get dm > "is this malware?" > "windows defender went crazy when i tried this video game mod" > download > look inside > not malware  There is a function in Windows you can invoke called "GetAsyncKeyState". In simplest terms, this function waits for user input on the keyboard. When a key is pressed down GetAsyncKeyState tells you what was pressed down.  GetAsyncKeyState is used frequently with video games. Using this function video games can determine ... what you're pressing. Likewise, GetAsyncKeyState can be used by legitimate software for when a user does a shortcut or something. There is a lot of legitimate use cases for this function.  However, GetAsyncKeyState is also abused because GetAsyncKeyState is tells you what key on the keyboard is pressed down, GetAsyncKeyState is used as a way to record what users are typing. Typically the data returned from GetAsyncKeyState is piped to a text file. Other times malware developers may use GetAsyncKeyState to store recorded key strokes in-memory and then send it out externally to a remote computer.  Regardless, this video game mod was invoking GetAsyncKeyState and filtering each key press to determine when the mod was triggered. In essence, it was scanning and waiting to determine if it is toggled on or off. Furthermore, after the mod was toggled on or off, it logged when it was turned off for debugging purposes.  From Windows Defenders perspective it saw an unsigned and unverified library being arbitrarily loaded into a video game and, once loaded, it was scanning user keys and subsequently writing something to a text file.  After bonking this video game mod with a stick we know it's not malware. However, this is a great example of false-positives in anti-malware services because, by all means, this mod does seemingly mimic something a malware payload would do.  But it's not malware. It's just a silly video game mod for Baldurs Gate 3

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-09 08:03 KST

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI Coding Agents(Claude Code, Cursor 등)를 악용하여 보안 규칙을 우회하거나, 존재하지 않는 패키지명을 생성해 개발자를 유도하는 HalluSquatting 공격을 전개하고 있습니다([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/new-hallusquatting-attack-could-trick.html)). 또한, 클라우드 환경에서 AI 워크플로우와 클라우드 취약점을 결합해 단 72시간 만에 침투하는 등 공격 속도가 비약적으로 빨라졌습니다([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/cloud-security/lone-attacker-ai-breach-aws-cloud-environment)). 공급망 공격 측면에서는 NPM 및 PyPI에 악성 SDK를 배포하여 자격 증명을 탈취하는 사례가 지속적으로 보고되고 있습니다([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/fake-paysafe-skrill-sdks-on-npm-and-pypi-steal-credentials/)).

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **China-linked APT (LapDogs campaign):** SOHO 라우터를 타겟으로 LongLeash, DogLeash, JarLeash 등 신규 백도어를 배포하며 툴킷을 확장 중([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/china-linked-apt-expands-arsenal-with-new-leash-backdoors/)).
- **Cavern Manticore:** 이란 연계 APT로, 모듈형 C2 프레임워크를 사용하여 이스라엘 등 주요 타겟을 공격([Check Point Research](https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/cavern-manticore-exposing-iran-linked-modular-c2-framework/)).
- **Armored Likho:** 러시아 타겟의 BusySnake Stealer 캠페인을 수행하며 AI 생성 로더와 Python 기반 악성코드를 활용([Securelist](https://securelist.com/tr/armored-likho-apt-with-busysnake-stealer/120292/)).
- **TAG-182:** 이란 연계 그룹으로, MarkiRAT을 이용해 대학 및 연구기관 대상 사이버 스파이 활동 수행([Recorded Future](https://www.recordedfuture.com/research/nexus-tag182-disseminates-markirat)).

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **피싱 메일 유포:** 카카오 계정 이전 안내를 사칭한 피싱 메일이 유포되어 사용자 자격 증명 탈취 시도([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94387/)).
- **국내 타겟 Vidar:** 국내 기업을 대상으로 한 Vidar Infostealer 유포 사례가 지속적으로 모니터링됨([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94362/)).
- **공급망 공격:** 북한 연계 해커가 Chai.js 플러그인으로 위장한 악성 npm 패키지(chai-as-init)를 배포한 사례 확인([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5766)).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-52831:** Nuclio OS command injection, CVSS 10.0, 악용 가능성 높음([SecAlertsCo](https://twitter.com/SecAlertsCo/status/2074985501774278786)).
- **CVE-2026-13019:** Esri Portal for ArcGIS 인증 우회, CVSS 9.8, 패치 미출시([HugoValters](https://twitter.com/HugoValters/status/2074984371786809703)).
- **CVE-2026-50043:** SkyBridge MB-A100/A110 OS Command Injection, CVSS 8.6([connect24h](https://twitter.com/connect24h/status/2074986981705285803)).
- **CVE-2026-20896:** Gitea Docker 관리자 권한 탈취, CVSS 미상(Critical), 활발히 악용 중([mergenewsapp](https://twitter.com/mergenewsapp/status/2074987453975351375)).
- **CVE-2026-50746:** UniFi Connect/Talk/Access/Protect 권한 상승 및 원격 코드 실행([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/ubiquiti-patches-critical-unifi-flaws.html)).

### 5. IOC 요약
- **CVE:** CVE-2026-50746 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/ubiquiti-patches-critical-unifi-flaws.html)), CVE-2026-52831 ([SecAlertsCo](https://twitter.com/SecAlertsCo/status/2074985501774278786)), CVE-2026-20896 ([mergenewsapp](https://twitter.com/mergenewsapp/status/2074987453975351375))
- **해시:** (이번 수집에서 특정 파일 해시값은 제공되지 않음)
- **도메인:** (이번 수집에서 특정 C2 도메인 정보는 제공되지 않음)

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **UniFi 장비 업데이트:** UniFi Connect, Talk, Access, Protect, OS 제품군에 대해 최신 보안 패치 적용(CVE-2026-50746 대응).
- **개발 환경 보안 강화:** AI Coding Agent 사용 시 외부 패키지 호출에 대한 검증 프로세스 도입 및 HalluSquatting 방지를 위한 의존성 패키지 무결성 확인.
- **피싱 대응:** 카카오 등 주요 서비스 사칭 메일에 대한 임직원 교육 강화 및 Entra ID 등 계정 관리
